eventMetadata.context_description: Grêmio holds a clear edge as home favorites against relegation-threatened Vasco da Gama in their July 17 Serie A clash at Arena do Grêmio, with trader consensus implying around 55-60% win probability reflecting Grêmio's unbeaten streak in the last five head-to-heads and solid sixth-place standing. events.title: Miguel Díaz-Canel out as leader of Cuba by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Cuba’s current leader, Miguel Díaz-Canel, is removed from power for any length of time by the listed date, ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

For the purposes of this market, Díaz-Canel will be considered to be removed from power if he resigns, is detained, or otherwise ceases to hold (or is publicly reported to be unable to perform the duties of) the office of First Secretary of the Communist Party of Cuba, which is widely regarded as Cuba’s top political post, within this market’s timeframe. 

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a low implied probability for Miguel Díaz-Canel's removal as Cuba's president soon, driven by the Cuban Communist Party's firm control despite deepening economic woes. Chronic blackouts, fuel shortages, and food scarcity—worsened by U.S. sanctions and Hurricane Rafael's recent devastation—have sparked public discontent and online criticism, echoing 2021 protests that were swiftly suppressed. However, no verified signs of internal party fractures, elite defections, or external interventions have emerged, with Díaz-Canel securely re-elected in 2023 for a term through 2028. Upcoming National Assembly sessions could signal policy shifts, but leadership stability remains the baseline amid regime resilience.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Republican Party to contest the GA-08 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Republican primary will take place on May 19, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican sources, including https://rnc.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Austin Scott's long-held incumbency since 2011 drives his commanding 93.5% trader consensus as GA-08 Republican primary winner, reinforced by dominant fundraising—over $1 million raised versus Vinson Watkins' modest totals—and a track record of easy primary victories in this solidly conservative rural district. Recent filings locked in a two-way race with no other challengers, reflecting low competition and trader confidence in status quo continuity. Realistic shifts could arise from a surprise endorsement for Watkins, such as from Trump allies, or an unexpected Scott scandal, though such catalysts remain improbable ahead of the May 21 ballot.

eventMetadata.context_description: Analilia Mejia's commanding 96% implied probability in the New Jersey 11th Legislative District special election stems from her double-digit lead in the latest public polls, bolstered by superior fundraising—over $400,000 raised versus Joe Hathaway's under $100,000—and strong Democratic voter registration edge in the district. The seat, vacated by Republican Brian Bergen amid a scandal, favors Democrats in a midterm environment with higher turnout from their base. Recent endorsements from labor unions and women's groups have solidified her position in this competitive Monmouth County district. Challenges could arise from unexpected GOP mobilization, low Democratic turnout, or a late-breaking Hathaway surge tied to local issues like property taxes, though traders see slim odds for reversal before November 5.

eventMetadata.context_description: Republican incumbent Tom McClintock dominated the CA-04 top-two primary on March 5, 2024, capturing 42.5% of the vote to advance alongside Democrat Mike Barkley at 24.1%, reflecting the district's strong Republican lean (Cook PVI R+11). Pre-election trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favored McClintock's incumbency advantage, superior fundraising over $1 million, and consistent polling leads from sources like Race to the WH, implying 75-90% probabilities for his top finish. Secondary contenders like Republican Kevin Hendrickson faded amid weak support. With the primary resolved, focus shifts to the November 5 general election matchup, where McClintock holds a clear edge per recent district-wide surveys and historical base rates in safe GOP seats. No further primaries scheduled.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment in the CT-01 Democratic primary heavily favors Luke Bronin at 46.5%, driven by a recent Emerson College poll showing him edging incumbent John Larson 36%-32% among likely voters, with 17% undecided ahead of the August 13 contest. Bronin's momentum stems from strong Q2 fundraising—over $450,000 raised versus Larson's lower totals—and his record as Hartford mayor appealing to moderates seeking change from Larson's 25-year tenure amid progressive critiques. Larson retains union and party establishment backing, holding steady at 33.5%, while lower odds for Ruth Fortune (6%), Jillian Gilchrest (4.5%), and Mark Stewart Greenstein (2.1%) reflect their limited poll support and resources, underscoring poll-driven uncertainty in this tight race.
